claim: For all integers n >= 1, the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n equals n.
predicate: all(sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is prime, then phi(n) = n - 1.
predicate: all((not is_prime(n)) or (phi(n) == n - 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is equal to n.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes p and q, then phi(n) = (p - 1) * (q - 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (phi(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) - 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then phi(n) = n / 2.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (phi(n) == n // 2) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of three distinct primes, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is equal to n.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 3) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 3, then phi(n) = 2 * n / 3.
predicate: all((not all(vp(n, p) == 0 for p in range(2, n) if p != 3 and is_prime(p))) or (vp(n, 3) == 0) or (phi(n) == 2 * n // 3) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a semiprime (product of two primes, not necessarily distinct), then phi(n) = n - p - q + 1, where p and q are the prime factors of n.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (phi(n) == n - sum([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then phi(n) = n - n / p.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0]) != 1) or (phi(n) == n - n // min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0])) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is square-free, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is equal to n.
predicate: all((any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p))) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of a prime p, then the sum of phi(d) over all positive divisors d of n is equal to n.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0]) != 1) or (sum(phi(d) for d in range(1, n + 1) if n % d == 0) == n) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is square-free, then phi(n) is the product of (p - 1) for each distinct prime p dividing n.
predicate: all((any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p))) or (phi(n) == math.prod([p - 1 for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a prime power p^k, then the sum of the divisors of n is (p^(k+1) - 1) / (p - 1).
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0]) != 1) or (sigma(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0]) ** (vp(n, min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0])) + 1) - 1) // (min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and vp(n, p) > 0]) - 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is square-free, then the sum of the divisors of n is the product of (p + 1) for each distinct prime p dividing n.
predicate: all((any(vp(n, p) > 1 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p))) or (sigma(n) == math.prod([p + 1 for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is a power of 2, then the sum of the divisors of n is 2^(k+1) - 1, where k is the exponent of 2 in n.
predicate: all((n & (n - 1) != 0) or (sigma(n) == (2 ** (vp(n, 2) + 1) - 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is the product of two distinct primes, then the sum of the divisors of n is (p + 1) * (q + 1), where p and q are the distinct prime factors of n.
predicate: all((len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (sigma(n) == (min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1) * (max([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) + 1)) for n in range(1, 501))

claim: For all integers n >= 1, if n is even and square-free, then the sum of the divisors of n is divisible by 3.
